\# Semester Project (React) — Assignment 1

\#\# Project Proposal  
My semester project is a web application called \*\*Movie Explorer\*\*. The purpose of the app is to help users browse movies and keep a simple list of favorites in one place. The target audience is students and movie lovers who want a clean and easy interface without complicated features.

The problem this application solves is that people often forget what they planned to watch, and it is inconvenient to keep notes in different places. The Minimum Viable Product (MVP) for the first stage includes a \*\*Home page\*\*, basic layout (Header/MainContent/Footer), and a clean project structure that will be expanded during the semester (pages, components, assets).

\#\# SPA Theory Questions

\#\#\# 1\) What is a Single Page Application (SPA)?  
A Single Page Application is a web app that loads a single HTML page once and then updates the content dynamically as the user interacts with it. Instead of reloading the entire page, JavaScript changes only the necessary parts of the UI. This usually makes navigation feel faster and smoother. SPAs often fetch data from an API in the background. React is commonly used to build SPAs because it handles UI updates efficiently.

\#\#\# 2\) How does SPA differ from traditional Multi-Page Applications (MPA)?  
In an MPA, each navigation action typically loads a new HTML page from the server, which causes a full page reload. In an SPA, the app stays on one page and updates the view in the browser without a full reload. MPAs can be simpler for SEO and initial load, while SPAs often provide a smoother user experience after loading. SPAs rely more on JavaScript in the browser and usually use client-side routing.

\#\#\# 3\) What is the Virtual DOM?  
The Virtual DOM is a lightweight copy of the real browser DOM stored in memory. When state changes, React creates a new Virtual DOM tree and compares it with the previous one. React then updates only the parts of the real DOM that actually changed. This reduces expensive direct DOM operations. As a result, updates are usually faster and more efficient.

\#\#\# 4\) Why does React use a component-based architecture?  
Component-based architecture breaks the UI into small, reusable pieces called components. Each component is responsible for a specific part of the interface, which makes the project easier to manage. Components can be reused in multiple places, reducing duplicated code. This structure also improves readability and teamwork because different people can work on separate components. In React, components help build complex UIs in a clean and organized way.

\#\# How to Run  
1\. Install dependencies: \`npm install\`  
2\. Start the app: \`npm start\`  
3\. Open: \`http://localhost:3000\`
